concerns.text: The plan explicitly rejects the 'COSMOS' dataset listed in the verified block as automotive and unrelated to binary black hole mergers. However, it fails to identify a valid, accessible alternative source for the required 'dedicated binary black hole population synthesis catalog' (FR-002). The fallback strategy of generating synthetic data 'only if no external download succeeds' contradicts the requirement to download a real population synthesis catalog for statistical comparison. Without a verified, appropriate dataset source for the simulation data, the core statistical analysis (KS tests on mass_ratio/effective_spin) cannot be performed as specified.

The plan acknowledges 'NO verified source found' for GWTC-1 (DOI 10.5281/zenodo.3966973) and GWTC-2 in the prompt's verified block, yet proceeds with the assumption that the DOIs are valid. While the plan includes error handling, the lack of a confirmed, appropriate source for the primary observational dataset (GWTC) creates a high risk that the pipeline will fail the 'Independent Test' for data acquisition (US-1) before any analysis can occur. The plan must either confirm a valid source or explicitly pivot the research question to a simulation-only comparison if observational data is unavailable.
